name: Math POP
description: The Math POP takes one attribute of its input, does some math operations, and outputs to any existing attribute or a new attribute. The attribute you specify can be any type, including float2, float3, float4. For Input Attribute Scope you can specify a subset of the components, like P(1) P(0) which has the effect of re-ordering Y and X. Math operation include simple Multiply and Add, as well as 30+ math functions including the trigonometry functions, exponential, log, power, and combining multiple components of an attribute into one like length(). The result can then be re-ranged (see the standalone [[ReRange POP]] and quantized (see the standalone [[Quantize POP]]). If you set Parameter Size to 3 and your attribute is a float3 like P, then for each component of P you can have separate Multiply, Add and Operation parameters. The Math POP operates on either point, vertex or primitive attributes. Angle Units can be specified in degrees, radians or rotations (cycles). One cycle is 360 degrees or 6.283 (2 Pi) radians. When creating new attribute, the Math POP will determine what data type it will be, but you can override it with the >>> expansion parameters. You can cause the output parameter to be integers. See also [[Math Mix POP]], [[Math Combine POP]], [[ReRange POP]], [[Quantize POP]]

parameters.name: Attribute Class
parameters.label: Attribute Class
parameters.description: Makes the POP operate on point attributes, vertex attributes or primitive attributes where applicable.

parameters.name: Group
parameters.label: Group
parameters.description: If there are input groups, specifying a group name in this field will cause this POP to act only upon the group specified.

parameters.name: Input Attribute Scope
parameters.label: Input Attribute Scope
parameters.description: Input's attributes you want to affect within the chosen attribute class, or attribute components.

parameters.name: Angle Units
parameters.label: Angle Units
parameters.description: Any angles in parameters are expressed in degrees (0-360 is one rotation), radians (0-2*pi) or cycles (0-1 is one rotation).

parameters.name: Parameter Size
parameters.label: Parameter Size
parameters.description: Number of independent configurable parameter values.

parameters.name: Pre Operation
parameters.label: Pre Operation
parameters.description: Math operation before the add-multiply-add.

parameters.name: Pre Add
parameters.label: Pre Add
parameters.description: Value to add before the multiply.

parameters.name: Multiply
parameters.label: Multiply
parameters.description: Multiply by this value.

parameters.name: Post Add
parameters.label: Post Add
parameters.description: Value to add after the multiply.

parameters.name: Post Operation
parameters.label: Post Operation
parameters.description: Math operation after the add-multiply-add.

parameters.name: Map from Low
parameters.label: Map from Low
parameters.description: Reranges the attribute value.

parameters.name: Map from High
parameters.label: Map from High
parameters.description: Reranges the attribute value.

parameters.name: Map to Low
parameters.label: Map to Low
parameters.description: Reranges the attribute value.

parameters.name: Map to High
parameters.label: Map to High
parameters.description: Reranges the attribute value.

parameters.name: Quantize
parameters.label: Quantize
parameters.description: Convert values into a finite set of discrete levels.

parameters.name: Cast to
parameters.label: Cast to
parameters.description: Allows to cast the output attribute to a different type if wanted.

parameters.name: Output Attribute Scope
parameters.label: Output Attribute Scope
parameters.description: Name of attribute to output (can choose components of attribute), can choose from menu.

parameters.name: Override Automatic Attribute
parameters.label: Override Automatic Attribute
parameters.description: Whether to override the kind of attribute automatically created based on the POP input and parameters. Allows to specify manually the type and number of components of the new attribute.

parameters.name: Attribute Type
parameters.label: Attribute Type
parameters.description: The output attribute's data type, default float.

parameters.name: Components
parameters.label: Components
parameters.description: The number of components in the new custom attribute.

parameters.name: Default Value
parameters.label: Default Value
parameters.description: Default values of the output attribute components if they cannot be computed.
